hypothesis: Introducing a controlled low cross-link density (approximately one trifunctional cross-link per 25–35 backbone atoms) into the linear polyamine architectures studied here will increase simulated CO2 uptake at 298 K and 0.4 mbar by at least 20% relative to their linear analogs by stabilizing subnanometer free volume, whereas increasing cross-link density beyond one per 10 backbone atoms will decrease uptake by at least 10% due to amine occlusion.
